nodes.name: Check CBN Updates
nodes.notes: The scraper itself waits for content to stabilize (can take 15-30s per page), so this node needs a generous timeout — 60s, not the default.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO cbn_documents (url, title, sources)
VALUES (
  '{{ $json.url.replace(/'/g, "''") }}',
  '{{ $json.title.replace(/'/g, "''") }}',
  '{{ JSON.stringify($json.sources).replace(/'/g, "''") }}'::jsonb
)
ON CONFLICT (url) DO NOTHING
RETURNING id, url, title, sources;

nodes.name: Upsert Documents
nodes.notes: Query Batching MUST be set to Independent (Options > Query Batching), or every item gets merged into one opaque batch call and you lose per-item RETURNING data — this exact bug cost real debugging time in Project 1.

nodes.name: Is New?
nodes.notes: A real 'id' means the INSERT actually happened (genuinely new document). A conflict (already seen) returns no RETURNING data, so id will be empty on that branch.

nodes.name: Mark as Notified
nodes.notes: Sits downstream of 'Collect All New IDs', not Telegram — deliberately decoupled so this doesn't depend on Telegram succeeding, and so it can use plain $json.ids with no cross-node reference at all.
